test: Method-transfer generalization: re-run directional nomination framework on an INDEPENDENT CRISPRi assay (Schmidt 2022 Dolcetto cytokine-sort screen) vs the Perturb-seq pipeline
caveat: Still CD4+ and partly shared lab lineage (Marson); the generalization demonstrated is across ASSAY MODALITY (pooled cytokine-sort screen vs Perturb-seq), not cell type. True CD8/macrophage generalization not feasible: Schmidt genome-wide GEO deposit is CD4-only; independent CD8/macrophage cytokine screens are mouse or paywalled; local RAM/disk cannot process a new single-cell Perturb-seq.
verdict: The nomination FRAMEWORK transfers across assay modality: an independent CRISPRi cytokine-sort screen recovers known targets at the same AUROC (0.57) as the Perturb-seq causal axis, and 26/50 top nominations are shared (p=3e-31, hypergeometric). Directional agreement is moderate (59%), consistent with the calibration finding that per-gene direction has modest resolution. Deep-dive nominations CTSH, SIK2, PTPN2 recur independently.
